description: Ambrose is a Mexican gang from Chicago's south side that expanded significantly from the 1980s onward to multiple neighborhoods in Chicago, suburbs, and cities like San Antonio, Aurora, Rockford, and beyond. The gang was known for violent territorial conflicts with numerous rivals and eventually joined the Folks alliance in 1994 after being sponsored by the Simon City Royals. Ambrose became particularly dominant in Marquette Park and established themselves as one of the larger gangs in the Chicago area by the 1990s.

In the year 1973, the Gangster Disciples (known as Black Gangster Disciples at the time) were looking to establish business with gangs that were not of the African American race. Black Gangster Disciples connected with the Ashland Vikings, a mostly Puerto Rican group, the Simon City Royals, a mostly white group and Ambrose, a mostly Mexican group.
Black Gangster Disciples connected with the Ashland Vikings, a mostly Puerto Rican group, the Simon City Royals, a mostly white group and Ambrose, a mostly Mexican group.
In the year 1974, the first incident of violence erupted between the two groups
Both Ambrose and Satan Disciples would become part of the Folks alliance in the late 70s/early 80s and would even form the "ATSD" which stood for "Ambrose Two Six and Satan Disciples.
Ambrose and Two Six became great allies when Two Six formed in 1970 and this alliance would continue into the 80s when both gangs joined Folks and the ATSD was effective.
War broke out in 1986 when Two Six gunned down Two Two Boys and Satan Disciples at a Ambrose party even though Ambrose was the target.

Two Two Boys and Ambrose became very close in the midst of all this which turned them into "cousins" on the streets. Two Two Boys and Ambrose didn't have any official relations like "Insane" or "Gangster" because Ambrose did not want to be bound to anything besides Folks, Two Boys were the only group that respected that; therefore, they had a very tight relationship for many years.

Reno "Maniac Mouse" Enriquez of the Latin Counts from address 1619 S. Loomis (17 th and Loomis) shot Louie Limas to death when Enriquez was only 17 years old. The death of Louie Limas was devastating and caused a lot of anger from Ambrose toward Latin Counts, the war intensified since Limas' death.
